cr_type.rationale: The canonical ledger ('chain') is a distinct concern from block proposal and needs its own governance boundary; it is not an extension of an existing subdomain.

business_vocabulary.definition: The authoritative, ordered, append-only ledger of committed blocks (also called the canonical chain or canonical ledger).
A unit of the ledger produced by a proposer and recorded on the chain; carries the transactions of its round.
A block produced by a proposer in the consensus loop, not yet committed and not yet authoritative.

To make a proposed block part of the canonical chain: its content is hashed as its signature, it is linked to its predecessor, and it is recorded as canonical. Commit is irreversible; on commit the block and its contained transactions become immutable and authoritative.
The chain's first block. It has the same form as any block and contains the first system transaction — a mint crediting the mint wallet — performed by the Genesis Actor.
The one-time genesis sequence that establishes the initial chain and supply, before the consensus loop runs.
The special, permanent actor that receives the initial minted supply at bootstrap and owns the mint wallet thereafter.
The validator selected to produce a block in a given round.
The system's unit of value; the supply is a closed monetary system.

requested_outcomes.outcome: Establish a closed, canonical chain (ledger) that commits proposer-produced blocks to an authoritative, append-only record.
Bootstrap the chain from a genesis block that mints the initial supply to a Genesis Actor before the consensus loop runs.
For this increment, commit all proposed blocks directly to the chain, emulating the proof-of-stake model with attestation and finalization deferred to future iterations.

known_facts.fact: A canonical chain (ledger) is required as the authoritative record of committed blocks.
A genesis bootstrap is required, and must occur before consensus execution begins.
The genesis block shall mint an initial supply of 1,000,000 BachiCoin.
The initial supply shall be assigned to a Genesis Actor, which is permanent and owns the mint wallet.
Minting occurs only during genesis bootstrap; no minting and no burning occur in this release.
For this development increment, all proposer-produced blocks are committed to the chain.
Attestation and finalization are intentionally deferred to a future iteration.
Consensus proposes; the chain records and is the authoritative source of committed history.
In this release, the chain commits every proposed block without additional validation.
On commit, a block and its contained transactions become authoritative and immutable.
Wallet balances are derived from committed transactions and reconciled on the chain as a post-commit reconciliation process; the chain does not maintain independent balance state.
known_facts.source_finding: CR seed §4 Known Facts #11 (human decision, S2 design review)

system_beliefs.belief: The current implementation does not yet provide a chain that commits proposed blocks.
system_beliefs.why_it_matters: This CR exists to fill that gap; if a commit capability already exists, the CR scope changes.
system_beliefs.verification_goal: Confirm no existing capability commits proposed blocks to a ledger.

system_beliefs.belief: A consensus loop already exists that proposes blocks and drives slot processing.
system_beliefs.why_it_matters: The chain commits exactly the blocks this loop proposes — its upstream producer.
system_beliefs.verification_goal: Identify the governing workflow(s), their producers, the records emitted, and the owning subdomain.

system_beliefs.why_it_matters: Defines the input the chain commit consumes.
system_beliefs.verification_goal: Identify the capability that produces a proposed block and the record/shape it emits.

system_beliefs.why_it_matters: Validators feed proposer selection upstream of the chain.
system_beliefs.verification_goal: Identify where validators are registered and how proposer selection reads it.

system_beliefs.why_it_matters: Genesis mints the initial supply to the mint wallet held by the Genesis Actor.
system_beliefs.verification_goal: Identify the wallet capability and how a balance/mint is recorded.

system_beliefs.why_it_matters: Part of the pipeline actor to wallet to transaction to mempool.
system_beliefs.verification_goal: Identify the transaction capability and its place in that pipeline.

system_beliefs.why_it_matters: Transactions queue there before block formation.
system_beliefs.verification_goal: Identify the mempool store and how transactions queue before a block is formed.

system_beliefs.why_it_matters: Drives slot processing and the consensus loop.
system_beliefs.verification_goal: Identify the orchestration/slot-processing driver and what it invokes.

system_beliefs.belief: Adjacent subdomains exist: identity, wallet, transaction, mempool, consensus, orchestration.
system_beliefs.why_it_matters: Establishes the neighborhood the new chain subdomain plugs into.
system_beliefs.verification_goal: Confirm each named subdomain exists and note its owning boundary.

assumptions.assumption: All proposed blocks are good and are committed as finalized (no rejection path this increment).
assumptions.basis: Incremental-development decision; attestation and finalization deferred.

constraints.constraint: Closed monetary system — no supply enters or leaves except by the system's own rules.
The chain is immutable — a committed block cannot be altered or removed.
Genesis supply is fixed at 1,000,000 BachiCoin, minted to the Genesis Actor at bootstrap.

business_invariants.invariant: Exactly one genesis block exists per chain.
Genesis executes exactly once at bootstrap and is never replayed.
Total supply is conserved and equals 1,000,000 BachiCoin (no minting or burning after genesis).
A committed block is immutable — it never changes or disappears.
Every committed block has exactly one predecessor, except the genesis block.
A block cannot be committed twice.
The canonical chain is the authoritative source of committed history; a proposed block is not authoritative until committed.

lifecycle_states.meaning: No genesis block yet; the chain is not established.
Genesis created; the chain accepts and commits blocks.
Produced by a proposer; not yet committed and not authoritative.
Recorded in the canonical chain; immutable and authoritative.
The single first block, established at bootstrap; permanent.

business_events.when_it_occurs: Once, at bootstrap, before the consensus loop runs.
business_events.significance: Establishes the chain and the initial monetary state.

business_events.when_it_occurs: When a proposer produces a block in a round.
business_events.significance: A candidate block exists; not yet authoritative.

business_events.when_it_occurs: When a proposed block is committed to the canonical chain.
business_events.significance: The block and its contained transactions become authoritative and immutable.

business_events.when_it_occurs: After a block is committed, when wallet balances are recomputed from the committed transactions.
business_events.significance: Wallet balances become consistent with the canonical committed history.
business_events.source_finding: CR seed §10 Business Events (human decision, S2 design review)

authority_boundaries.authoritative_owner: Chain (derived by post-commit reconciliation from committed transactions, not owned as independent state)
authority_boundaries.source_finding: CR seed §11 Authority Boundaries (human decision, S2 design review)

out_of_scope.item: The attest step of the proof-of-stake progression.
out_of_scope.reason: Deferred to a future iteration; all proposed blocks treated as good this increment.

out_of_scope.item: The finalize step of the proof-of-stake progression.
out_of_scope.reason: Deferred to a future iteration; proposed blocks committed directly.
Not part of this release; the chain commits every proposed block.
Not part of this release; committed history is immutable.
Validator penalties are out of scope this release.
Validator/proposer rewards are out of scope this release.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The chain begins with a genesis block that records the assignment of the initial 1,000,000 BachiCoin supply to the Genesis Actor.
Blocks accepted by the chain appear in the authoritative ledger in proposal order.
A block recorded in the ledger never changes or disappears.
The total recorded supply on the ledger is 1,000,000 BachiCoin, held initially by the Genesis Actor.
Once committed, a block and its contained transactions are treated as authoritative.
